title: Cosmos Burn Reveal — paper-burn content reveal (Canvas 2D noise field)
summary: A parchment 'paper' sheet burns away from a corner with a charred black + glowing orange ember edge, spreading along a torn-paper noise boundary to reveal the Cosmos content (logo + COSMOS wordmark on a Phantom starfield) beneath. The burn front follows a precomputed noise field biased toward the ignition corner. Pure Canvas 2D, zero dependencies, runs offline. Seamless loop: burn -> reveal -> hold -> the sheet magically re-forms.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html (no server/CDN); keep cosmos-logo.png beside it. INTERACTION (live): it auto-plays the burn/reform loop. KEY TECHNIQUES: (1) a per-pixel burn FIELD is precomputed once = fbm value-noise + distance-from-ignition-corner, normalised 0..1 — so low values (corner) burn first and the edge is irregular like torn paper; (2) each frame, for a progress p, every pixel compares x = field - p: x>EMBER intact parchment (with marbled paperTex), 0<x<EMBER scorching (parchment darkens + ember rim), -CHAR<x<0 glowing char that fades to alpha 0, x<-CHAR fully clear -> the content layer shows through; (3) the paper is rendered to an offscreen ImageData then drawImage-composited over a prebuilt content canvas (alpha blend); (4) ember colour is added additively near the front (HOT band). CAPTURE: ?auto=1 + window.__seek(t); progress(t) is a burn/hold/reform curve so p(0)==p(LOOP)==0 -> seamless; the GIF is captured from a START offset so frame 0 is the brand reveal. TUNE: EMBER/CHAR/HOT widths, ignition corner, fs (noise freq), parchment colour. BRAND: Phantom content; ember is the only warm accent.
